entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shift in global or regional climate patterns predicted to increase the number and severity of extreme rainfall events

entities.name: EXTREME RAINFALL EVENTS
entities.description: Severe precipitation occurrences, including shortfalls and surpluses, particularly in Sub-Saharan Africa

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: Geographic region predicted to experience an increase in the number and severity of extreme rainfall events

entities.name: CLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURAL TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Agricultural practices encouraged by development agencies to enhance productivity and resilience to climate change

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A specific climate-smart agricultural method tested for its impact on yields and climate resilience

entities.name: PRODUCTION
entities.description: The overall agricultural output affected by the adoption of Conservation Agriculture

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of agricultural systems to withstand and recover from climate-related shocks

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The location where panel data was analyzed to evaluate the performance of Conservation Agriculture

entities.name: YIELDS
entities.description: The agricultural output per unit area, which varies based on the farming method and rainfall conditions

entities.name: RAINFALL SHOCKS
entities.description: Extreme deviations in rainfall that negatively impact agricultural yields

entities.name: AVERAGE RAINFALL
entities.description: Normal precipitation levels during which Conservation Agriculture may show limited or negative effects on yields

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL
entities.description: Rainfall deficiency conditions where Conservation Agriculture provides higher yields than conventional farming

entities.name: HIGH RAINFALL
entities.description: Excessive precipitation conditions where Conservation Agriculture provides higher yields than conventional farming
